# Deployment & Operations Runbook

> AppManager — IIS 站点与 Windows 服务管理系统

## Prerequisites

- **Windows Server** (IIS and Windows Service management are Windows-only APIs)
- **.NET 8.0+ SDK** or runtime installed
- **SQL Server** (any edition) accessible from the deployment target
- **Administrator privileges** (required for IIS/Windows Service management operations)
- **OpenID Connect provider** (for production SSO authentication)

## Architecture Overview

```
┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                  AppManager.Web                    │
│  ASP.NET Core MVC (Kestrel)                      │
│  Port: 44303 (HTTPS, dev)                        │
│  Auth: Cookie (dev) / OIDC (prod)                │
│  Logging: Serilog → Console + File + SQL Server  │
├──────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                  SQL Server                        │
│  Database: AppManager                             │
│  Tables: Business + ABP System + SerilogLogs     │
└──────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

## Deployment Procedures

### First-Time Deployment

```bash
# 1. Publish the application
dotnet publish src/AppManager.Web -c Release -o ./publish

# 2. Configure appsettings.json for production
#    - Set ConnectionStrings:Default to production SQL Server
#    - Set App:SelfUrl to production URL
#    - Set SSO:Authority, SSO:ClientId, SSO:ClientSecret for OIDC
#    - Generate a new StringEncryption:DefaultPassPhrase

# 3. Generate production OpenIddict certificates
dotnet dev-certs https -v -ep openiddict.pfx -p <strong-password>
# Place openiddict.pfx in the publish directory
# Update AppManagerWebModule.cs certificate password

# 4. Copy to deployment target
#    Copy ./publish to target server (e.g., C:\AppManager\)

# 5. Run database migration (one-time)
dotnet AppManager.DbMigrator.dll

# 6. Start the application
dotnet AppManager.Web.dll
```

### Deployment Checklist

<!-- AUTO-GENERATED: deploy-checklist -->
- [ ] Production connection string configured (not LocalDB)
- [ ] `StringEncryption:DefaultPassPhrase` replaced with new secure value
- [ ] SSO configuration set (Authority, ClientId, ClientSecret)
- [ ] OpenIddict production certificate (`openiddict.pfx`) deployed
- [ ] Certificate password updated in code/config
- [ ] Seed admin password changed from `1q2w3E*`
- [ ] OIDC client secrets changed from development defaults
- [ ] `appsettings.secrets.json` is gitignored (not committed)
- [ ] ASPNETCORE_ENVIRONMENT set to `Production`
- [ ] HTTPS certificate configured for production URL
- [ ] Firewall rules allow application port
- [ ] Application runs with administrator privileges
<!-- END AUTO-GENERATED: deploy-checklist -->

### IIS Hosting (Alternative to Kestrel)

```xml
<!-- web.config for IIS hosting -->
<configuration>
  <system.webServer>
    <handlers>
      <add name="aspNetCore" path="*" verb="*"
           modules="AspNetCoreModuleV2" resourceType="Unspecified" />
    </handlers>
    <aspNetCore processPath="dotnet"
                arguments="AppManager.Web.dll"
                stdoutLogEnabled="false"
                stdoutLogFile=".\logs\stdout"
                hostingModel="inprocess">
      <environmentVariables>
        <environmentVariable name="ASPNETCORE_ENVIRONMENT" value="Production" />
      </environmentVariables>
    </aspNetCore>
  </system.webServer>
</configuration>
```

### Windows Service Hosting

To run as a Windows Service:

```bash
# Create a Windows Service for the app
sc create "AppManager" binPath="dotnet C:\AppManager\AppManager.Web.dll" start=auto

# Start the service
sc start AppManager
```

Or use `Microsoft.Extensions.Hosting.WindowsServices`:

```bash
dotnet add src/AppManager.Web package Microsoft.Extensions.Hosting.WindowsServices
```

Then in `Program.cs`:
```csharp
builder.Host.UseWindowsService();
```

## Configuration Guide

### Connection String

The application uses a single SQL Server connection string. In development, LocalDB with Windows Integrated Authentication is used. For production:

```json
{
  "ConnectionStrings": {
    "Default": "Server=prod-db.example.com;Database=AppManager;User Id=app_user;Password=<secure>;TrustServerCertificate=True"
  }
}
```

### Authentication Modes

| Environment | Mode | Configuration |
|-------------|------|---------------|
| Development | Cookie + Form Login | Default (no config needed) |
| Production | OpenID Connect SSO | Set `SSO:Authority`, `SSO:ClientId`, `SSO:ClientSecret` |

### Serilog Logging

Log output sinks configured in `Program.cs`:

| Sink | Path/Table | Configuration |
|------|------------|---------------|
| Console | stdout | Async, colored output |
| File | `Logs/logs.txt` | Async, append mode |
| SQL Server | `SerilogLogs` table | Async, batch 50/5s (architecture doc shows MSSqlServer sink) |

> **Note:** The current `Program.cs` code configures Console and File sinks only. The SQL Server sink shown in architecture docs requires adding `Serilog.Sinks.MSSqlServer` configuration.

### Seed Account

Default admin account seeded by `AppManager.DbMigrator`:

| Field | Default Value |
|-------|---------------|
| Username | `admin` |
| Email | `admin@default.com` |
| Password | `1q2w3E*` |

> ⚠️ **Change the seed admin password immediately** after first deployment.

## Health Check & Monitoring

### Application Health

The application does not currently expose a dedicated health check endpoint. To add health checks:

```csharp
// In AppManagerWebModule.cs or Program.cs
builder.Services.AddHealthChecks()
    .AddSqlServer(connectionString)
    .AddUrlGroup(new Uri(builder.Configuration["App:SelfUrl"]!), "Self");

app.UseEndpoints(endpoints =>
{
    endpoints.MapHealthChecks("/health");
});
```

### Monitoring Checklist

<!-- AUTO-GENERATED: monitoring -->
- [ ] Application process is running (`tasklist | findstr AppManager.Web`)
- [ ] Application responds on configured URL
- [ ] SQL Server is accessible from application server
- [ ] IIS/WMI services are accessible (for IIS/Windows Service management)
- [ ] Log files are being written (`Logs/logs.txt`)
- [ ] Disk space is sufficient for log files (30-day retention)
- [ ] `AbpAuditLogs` table size is monitored (audit log growth)
- [ ] OpenIddict token cleanup job runs regularly
<!-- END AUTO-GENERATED: monitoring -->

## Common Issues & Fixes

### Database Connection Failure

**Symptom:** `SqlException: A network-related or instance-specific error occurred`

**Fix:**
1. Verify SQL Server is running
2. Check connection string in `appsettings.json`
3. Ensure firewall allows SQL Server port (default 1433)
4. Verify Windows authentication or SQL login credentials

### Admin Privileges Required

**Symptom:** `UnauthorizedAccessException` when managing IIS sites or Windows Services

**Fix:**
- Run Visual Studio or the application as Administrator
- For IIS hosting: configure the application pool identity with appropriate permissions
- For Windows Service hosting: set the service to run as LocalSystem or a privileged account

### OpenIddict Certificate Error

**Symptom:** `InvalidOperationException: The signing credentials cannot be resolved`

**Fix:**
1. Generate new certificate: `dotnet dev-certs https -v -ep openiddict.pfx -p <password>`
2. Place `openiddict.pfx` in the application directory
3. Verify certificate password in `AppManagerWebModule.cs`

### Migration Failures

**Symptom:** Database migration fails with schema errors

**Fix:**
1. Ensure SQL Server is accessible
2. Verify connection string
3. Check if database already exists (might need manual cleanup)
4. Run DbMigrator in isolation: `dotnet run --project src/AppManager.DbMigrator`

### Port Already in Use

**Symptom:** `IOException: Failed to bind to address https://127.0.0.1:44303`

**Fix:**
1. Change `App:SelfUrl` in `appsettings.json`
2. Or kill the process using the port: `netstat -ano | findstr :44303`

## Backup & Restore Operations

### IIS Site Backup

The application stores IIS site configuration snapshots in the `AppIisSiteBackups` table. These are full JSON backups of site configuration including bindings, application pools, sub-applications, and virtual directories.

**Manual backup via API:**
```bash
# Create a backup
curl -X POST https://localhost:44303/api/app/iis-site-backup \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer <token>" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"siteName": "mysite", "description": "Pre-deployment backup"}'

# List backups
curl https://localhost:44303/api/app/iis-site-backup?siteName=mysite

# Restore a backup
curl -X POST https://localhost:44303/api/app/iis-site-backup/restore \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer <token>" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"backupId": "<guid>", "overwriteExisting": true}'
```

### Windows Service Backup

Service configurations are backed up to the `AppWindowsServiceBackups` table.

### Database Backup

Don't forget to also back up the SQL Server database itself:

```bash
# Using sqlcmd
sqlcmd -S <server> -Q "BACKUP DATABASE AppManager TO DISK='C:\Backups\AppManager.bak'"
```

## Rollback Procedures

### Application Rollback

```bash
# 1. Stop the application
# 2. Restore previous publish directory
# 3. Run any necessary down-migration
# 4. Restart the application
```

### IIS Site Restore Rollback

When restoring an IIS site from backup, the application automatically creates a **pre-restore snapshot** (stored in `AppIisSiteBackups`). To rollback:

1. Navigate to IIS Site Backups in the application UI
2. Find the auto-created pre-restore snapshot
3. Restore from that snapshot

### Database Rollback

EF Core migrations can be rolled back:

```bash
# Remove the last migration
dotnet ef migrations remove --project src/AppManager.EntityFrameworkCore

# Or use a specific migration target
dotnet ef database update <MigrationName> --project src/AppManager.EntityFrameworkCore
```
